# Lyrd complete agent documentation

Version channel: `next`
Core package: `@lyrd/core`
CLI package: `@lyrd/cli`
Documentation: https://seunjin.github.io/lyrd/
Repository: https://github.com/seunjin/lyrd

## 1. What Lyrd is

Lyrd is a React overlay intent system. It is not a component library and does not reimplement Dialog, AlertDialog, Drawer, Toast, focus management, portals, or accessibility primitives.

Lyrd owns:

- imperative Promise-based calls
- typed input and result contracts
- overlay session lifecycle
- the safe default modal queue
- deduplication for built-in recipes
- identity-based updates
- explicit parallel scheduling
- centralized dismissal

The application owns:

- JSX and CSS
- Base UI, Radix, shadcn, or custom primitive composition
- product copy and button labels
- error presentation
- animation and responsive behavior
- product-specific result types

Do not invent a built-in `overlay.toast()` API. Toast is an app-owned adapter generated by the CLI.

## 2. Requirements

- React 19
- React DOM 19
- Node.js 18 or later for the CLI
- a client-side React Provider mounted once at the application root

## 3. Install the safe base recipes

Run from the target application root:

```bash
pnpm dlx @lyrd/cli add overlay --verbose
```

The command installs stable `@lyrd/core` and `@base-ui/react`, creates `lyrd.json`, and creates app-owned files similar to:

```text
src/lyrd/overlay/
  alert.tsx
  confirm.tsx
  overlay-provider.tsx
  overlay.css
  index.ts
```

Existing files are never overwritten. Review the command output and mount the generated Provider exactly once.

### Vite React

Compose `AppOverlayProvider` around the application in `src/main.tsx` or `src/main.jsx`:

```tsx
import { StrictMode } from 'react'
import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'
import { AppOverlayProvider } from './lyrd/overlay/overlay-provider'

createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!).render(
  <StrictMode>
    <AppOverlayProvider>
      <App />
    </AppOverlayProvider>
  </StrictMode>,
)
```

### Next App Router

The CLI creates a client bridge such as `src/app/lyrd-overlay-provider.tsx`. Mount it once in `layout.tsx`:

```tsx
import { LyrdOverlayProvider } from './lyrd-overlay-provider'

export default function RootLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <html lang="ko">
      <body>
        <LyrdOverlayProvider>{children}</LyrdOverlayProvider>
      </body>
    </html>
  )
}
```

Do not call `useOverlay()` from a Server Component. Put calls in Client Components.

## 4. Access the central API

```tsx
import { useOverlay } from '@lyrd/core'

function Example() {
  const overlay = useOverlay()
}
```

`useOverlay()` must be called below `OverlayProvider` or the generated `AppOverlayProvider`.

## 5. Alert recipe

`overlay.alert(request)` returns `Promise<void>`.

```tsx
await overlay.alert({
  title: '저장을 완료했습니다.',
  description: '변경 사항이 서버에 반영됐습니다.',
  acknowledgeLabel: '확인',
  dedupeKey: 'project-saved',
})
```

Alert fields:

- `title: ReactNode` required
- `description?: ReactNode`
- `acknowledgeLabel?: ReactNode`
- `dedupeKey?: string` prevents duplicate active requests using the same key

## 6. Confirm recipe

`overlay.confirm(request)` returns `Promise<boolean>`.

```tsx
const confirmed = await overlay.confirm({
  title: '프로젝트를 삭제할까요?',
  description: '삭제한 프로젝트는 복구할 수 없습니다.',
  confirmLabel: '삭제',
  cancelLabel: '취소',
  tone: 'danger',
  dismissPolicy: 'block',
  dedupeKey: `delete-project:${projectId}`,
  onConfirm: async () => {
    await deleteProject(projectId)
  },
})

if (confirmed) {
  // onConfirm completed successfully.
}
```

Confirm fields:

- `title: ReactNode` required
- `confirmLabel: ReactNode` required
- `description?: ReactNode`
- `cancelLabel?: ReactNode`
- `tone?: 'neutral' | 'danger'`
- `dismissPolicy?: 'allow' | 'block'`
- `dedupeKey?: string`
- `onConfirm?: () => void | Promise<void>`

While an async `onConfirm` is pending, the generated renderer disables actions. Rejection moves the request to an error state and allows retry. The Promise resolves `true` only after `onConfirm` succeeds; cancellation resolves `false`.

## 7. Reusable typed custom overlays

Define input and result together. The component receives the latest input and a typed session.

```tsx
'use client'

import { Dialog } from '@base-ui/react/dialog'
import { defineOverlay } from '@lyrd/core'
import type { OverlayDefinitionComponentProps } from '@lyrd/core'

type ProjectSettingsInput = {
  projectId: string
  initialName: string
}

type ProjectSettingsResult = {
  projectName: string
  saved: true
}

type Props = OverlayDefinitionComponentProps<
  ProjectSettingsInput,
  ProjectSettingsResult
>

function ProjectSettingsOverlay({ input, session }: Props) {
  return (
    <Dialog.Root
      open={session.open}
      onOpenChange={(open, details) => {
        if (!open) {
          session.requestDismiss(details.reason === 'escape-key' ? 'escape' : 'outside')
        }
      }}
      onOpenChangeComplete={(open) => {
        if (!open) session.completeExit()
      }}
    >
      <Dialog.Portal>
        <Dialog.Backdrop />
        <Dialog.Popup>
          <Dialog.Title>프로젝트 설정</Dialog.Title>
          <ProjectSettingsForm
            initialName={input.initialName}
            projectId={input.projectId}
            onCancel={() => session.dismiss('cancel')}
            onSaved={(projectName) =>
              session.resolve({ saved: true, projectName })
            }
          />
        </Dialog.Popup>
      </Dialog.Portal>
    </Dialog.Root>
  )
}

export const projectSettings = defineOverlay(ProjectSettingsOverlay)
```

Open it and handle every outcome:

```tsx
const outcome = await overlay.open(projectSettings, {
  projectId,
  initialName,
})

if (outcome.status === 'resolved') {
  console.log(outcome.value.projectName)
} else {
  console.log(outcome.reason)
}
```

`OverlayOutcome<Result>` is:

```ts
type OverlayOutcome<Result> =
  | { status: 'resolved'; value: Result }
  | {
      status: 'dismissed'
      reason: 'cancel' | 'escape' | 'outside' | 'route-change' | 'programmatic'
    }
```

`overlay.open()` returns an `OverlayHandle<Input, Result>`. The handle is a real Promise, so simple callers can await it directly. Callers that retain it can update or explicitly dismiss that exact active session:

```tsx
const settings = overlay.open(projectSettings, { projectId, initialName })

settings.update({ projectId, initialName: nextName })
settings.dismiss('programmatic')

const outcome = await settings
```

`handle.update()` and `handle.dismiss()` return `false` after the session settles and never create another session.
`handle.dismiss()` is an explicit application action and bypasses `dismissPolicy`; primitive-originated ESC and outside attempts must still use Renderer `requestDismiss()`. Retain the direct return value from `open()` when control is needed because `.then()` returns a plain Promise.

Application API:

- `overlay.alert(request)` and `overlay.confirm(request)` provide safe product defaults
- `overlay.open(definition, input, options)` always creates a new typed session and returns an awaitable handle
- `overlay.openOrUpdate(definition, identity, input, options)` creates or updates an identity-bound session and returns its handle
- `overlay.dismissAll(reason)` cleans up the default queue and every custom group

Renderer API for definition and adapter authors:

- `resolve(result)` records a typed result and starts closing
- `dismiss(reason)` records a dismissal and starts closing
- `requestDismiss(reason)` honors `dismissPolicy: 'allow' | 'block'`
- `completeExit()` must be called when the primitive reports its exit transition completed
- `open` is the controlled open state
- `status` is `mounting`, `open`, or `closing`

Application callers normally do not use the Renderer API. A local Base UI, Radix, or shadcn adapter connects these methods once inside its overlay definition.

Use `pnpm dlx @lyrd/cli add dialog project-settings` to generate a Base UI Dialog starting point. The name must be kebab-case.

## 8. Identity-based updates with openOrUpdate

Use `openOrUpdate()` only when multiple call sites must find the same logical operation by a stable identity. Retain the returned handle when one local flow only needs to update its exact session.

```tsx
const completion = overlay.openOrUpdate(
  uploadProgress,
  uploadId,
  { uploadId, uploadedBytes: 0, totalBytes },
  { dismissPolicy: 'block' },
)

completion.update({
  uploadId,
  uploadedBytes,
  totalBytes,
})

const outcome = await completion
```

Rules:

- identity matching is scoped to the same overlay definition
- the active handle and component instance are reused
- input is updated to the latest value
- omitted options keep the active session's original options
- a completed or closing session is not reused
- handle updates never create a replacement session after settlement
- use `open()` for ordinary repeated dialogs

## 9. Parallel overlays and the Toast starter

The default queue is correct for modal overlays. Use an explicit parallel group only when independent overlays must render without waiting.

Generate the app-owned Toast adapter:

```bash
pnpm dlx @lyrd/cli add toast --verbose
```

Generated files:

```text
src/lyrd/overlay/
  toast-definition.ts
  toast.tsx
  toast-group.ts
  notify.ts
  toast.css
```

Compose Providers once:

```tsx
<AppToastProvider>
  <AppOverlayProvider>{children}</AppOverlayProvider>
</AppToastProvider>
```

Use the generated helper for ordinary calls. It owns default ID generation and selects the parallel group:

```tsx
notify(overlay, {
  title: '변경 사항을 저장했습니다.',
})

const action = await notifyWithUndo(overlay, {
  title: '항목을 삭제했습니다.',
  description: '필요하면 실행 취소할 수 있습니다.',
})

if (action === 'undo') {
  await undoSave()
}
```

The raw `overlay.open(appToast, input, { group: toastGroup })` API remains available for product-specific helpers. Undo is a resolved action. Timeout, swipe, close, and route cleanup settle as dismissed outcomes. Base UI 1.6 does not expose a specific timeout or swipe reason through `onClose`, so the starter uses `programmatic` as the fallback reason.

The generated Toast Provider defaults to a limit of 5 and timeout of 5000 ms. Base UI marks excess items with `data-limited`; the generated CSS hides them. These are app-owned defaults and may be changed locally.

## 10. Escape hatch and centralized cleanup

For one-off JSX or migration code only:

```tsx
const result = await overlay.dialog<string>(<LegacyDialog />)
```

`dialog()` returns `Result | undefined`. Prefer `defineOverlay()` for reusable typed behavior.

Dismiss all active default and parallel sessions during navigation or application cleanup:

```tsx
overlay.dismissAll('route-change')
```

Allowed reasons for `dismissAll()` are `route-change` and `programmatic`.

## 11. Scheduling and ownership rules

- `alert`, `confirm`, and `open` without a group use the safe modal queue
- every `open()` call creates an independent session
- `openOrUpdate()` is the only identity-based reuse path
- `parallel` is opt-in and currently the only group strategy
- do not mix multiple UI primitive implementations into core
- edit generated adapter files inside the application when product UX differs
- do not modify existing application entry files automatically; follow CLI output and integrate deliberately

## 12. Integration verification checklist

After installation, verify all applicable items:

1. production build and typecheck succeed
2. Provider is mounted once and hooks do not throw
3. Alert acknowledges and resolves
4. Confirm resolves `false` on cancel and `true` after successful confirmation
5. async Confirm displays pending and permits retry after rejection
6. custom overlay returns a resolved value and a dismissed reason
7. two ordinary modal calls queue instead of overlapping
8. `openOrUpdate()` preserves one component instance and awaitable Handle while input changes
9. multiple Toasts render in parallel without blocking the modal queue
10. route cleanup dismisses active overlays
11. generated application files remain app-owned and are not overwritten on a repeated CLI command

Use https://seunjin.github.io/lyrd/agent-test-prompt.md for a ready-to-run agent assignment.
